framework_source: NIST AI 100-1: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework

use_case_per_ir8477_section3.target_audience: WIDAI framework developers, AI risk management practitioners, and stakeholders evaluating WIDAI's coverage of AI risk management workforce competencies. Secondary audience: organizations building AI governance programs aligned with NIST AI RMF.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.why_this_mapping: NIST AI RMF 1.0 is the US federal government's primary AI risk management framework. It is the second regulatory/standards framework in the STRM sequence (after EU AI Act) and provides US-centric risk management perspective complementing the EU regulatory perspective of STRM-005. The AI RMF defines organizational outcomes across four functions (GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE) that collectively describe the competencies needed for trustworthy AI risk management. This STRM validates WIDAI coverage against a framework that shares vocabulary with WIDAI's governance and risk management domains but approaches competency from an outcome-based rather than task-based perspective.

concept_types: Subcategory outcome statements across 4 functions and 19 categories — 70 elements
focal_document.exclusion_rationale: No elements excluded. All 70 subcategory outcome statements evaluated. The AI RMF Core is entirely within WIDAI's scope — every subcategory describes an outcome relevant to data/AI workforce competency.

concept_types_mapped.structural_note: The NIST AI RMF uses a function / category / subcategory hierarchy where subcategories are outcome statements (e.g., 'Roles and responsibilities related to AI risks are documented and clear'). These outcome statements are more abstract than NICE/DCWF KSATs but more concrete than EU AI Act obligations. The competency_implication field translates each outcome into workforce competency language for scoring.

use_case_per_ir8477_section3.direction_of_mapping: Focal → Reference. Each NIST AI RMF subcategory outcome (focal) is evaluated against the WIDAI KSA pool (reference). A subcategory with 'No relationship' to any WIDAI KSA would indicate a gap in WIDAI's coverage of AI risk management workforce competencies.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.exhaustiveness_level: Exhaustive. Every NIST AI RMF subcategory outcome (70) is evaluated. For each element, the most semantically relevant WIDAI KSA is identified via bi-encoder candidate identification, then scored through the full multi-method pipeline (identical to STRM-001 through STRM-005).
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.rationale_type: Functional (primary). The mapping direction is outcome→competency: what workforce capability is required to achieve this risk management outcome? This is a Functional question per NIST IR 8477 Section 4.3, consistent with the STRM-005 EU AI Act methodology.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.rationale_justification: The NIST AI RMF describes organizational outcomes, not workforce competencies directly. WIDAI defines workforce competencies. The question 'what competency achieves this outcome' is Functional — it asks about the results of executing the mapped concepts. The competency_implication field bridges the vocabulary gap between outcome language and competency language, enabling STS-based scoring.

scope_decisions.note: All 70 subcategory outcomes evaluated without categorical exclusions. Zero 'No relationship' results — every NIST AI RMF subcategory maps to at least one WIDAI KSA, reflecting comprehensive coverage of AI risk management competencies by the WIDAI pool.

structural_elements_excluded.functions: Function-level descriptions (GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE) are organizational groupings, not FDEs.
structural_elements_excluded.categories: Category-level descriptions are thematic groupings, not outcome statements.
structural_elements_excluded.informative_references: The AI RMF Playbook's suggested actions and informative references are implementation guidance, not framework elements.

candidate_identification.description: Bi-encoder embeddings (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) used to identify the most semantically relevant WIDAI KSA candidate for each NIST AI RMF outcome element. Preprocessing step for pairing, not a scoring method.
candidate_identification.note: Candidate identification determines which WIDAI KSA each outcome element is paired with. All scoring, classification, and significance generation performed by strm/nist_ai_rmf/strm_scoring_pipeline.py, methodologically identical to STRM-001 through STRM-005.
